po_authorization_ref: Product-Owner explicit acceptance and closure of Phase 1B at verified DARK maturity (decision D11, 2026-07-17). Accepted HEAD 1594c805e2a6bdf2371b742999607b22a436a0ff; base master 47330a73b5875b9336e04fe74d3cf8040e6886ee; Governance CI run 29617730173 SUCCESS; deployment transition T0010 (2026-07-17T21:18:26Z).
scope_allowed: Record Phase 1B ACCEPTED_AND_CLOSED at DARK maturity and merge PR #2 into master as governance/code delivery only (no live appliance mutation). The accepted delivery: Gate-P least-privilege DB-role cutover (all four site-DB daemons on non-superuser svc_* roles), migrations 0007/0008, durable-throttle + keyed-HMAC OTP deployed DARK, protected local key lifecycle + OTP gen-1 bootstrap, dark IAM-v2 credential/identity/device/auth-context layer, complete software gate, live-dark deployment + final reboot verification, zero runtime iam_v2 privileges, iam_v2 49 tables/0 rows, legacy public-schema auth sole authority, evidence + governance reconciliation. The documented residual svc_scd privilege breadth is accepted as a known limitation with the recorded future-hardening recommendation.
scope_prohibited: This acceptance does NOT authorize: enabling durable throttle; enabling keyed-HMAC OTP; enabling any IAM-v2 method; any IAM-v2 production read or write; IAM-v2 cutover; dual read/dual write; legacy IAM removal; data migration; Phase 2 implementation; PMS/FIAS traffic; financial posting; paid guest access; network or HA changes. Every dark feature must remain OFF. Phase 2 remains NOT_STARTED and unauthorized; the system must await explicit Product-Owner authorization for any next phase.
acceptance_verdict: ACCEPTED_AND_CLOSED — Product Owner formally accepted and closed Phase 1B at verified DARK maturity via decision D11. Not a cutover authorization.
next_action: No next-phase implementation is authorized. Await explicit Product-Owner authorization before beginning any subsequent phase or enabling any dark feature.
